english: two words, viz. bde-ba (bde ba) and gšegs-pa (gshegs pa) Lex.; ₒdri-bai tsig ('dri ba'i tshig) interrogative (word), such as či (ci); tsig sgrig-pa (tshig sgrig pa) to connect or arrange words; as a sbst.: construction, the order in which words are to be placed; grammatical form, dá-ltar-gyi tsig (da ltar gyi tshig) form of the present tense; tsig-grógs (tshig grogs), tsig-grógs-kyi dbáṅ-gis (tshig grogs kyi dbang gis) Tar.; Schf.: 'by the force of construction' (?) tsig-ₒgrél (tshig 'grel) Tar. explanation of words; tsig-ₒgrós (tshig 'gros) Sch.: 'course of speech, connexion of words'; tsig-prád (tshig phrad), tsig-rgyán (tshig rgyan) particle, a small word not inflected; tsig-ₒbrú (tshig 'bru) Schr.: a separate word or syllable, tsig-ₒbrú-gñer-pa (tshig 'bru gnyer pa) Sch. 'linguist, philologist, purist'; tsig-ₒbru-lčibs (tshig 'bru lcibs) Lex.? — 2. word, saying, speech, subject of a discourse, tsig-snyán(-pa) (tshig snyan (-pa)) kind word, friendly speech, tsig-ₒjám (tshig 'jam) id., brtse-bai tsig (brtse ba'i tshig) an affectionate word Glr.; *tsig-súb* (*tshig sub*) W. hard, angry, bad words; *tsig-nán* (*tshig nan*), *tsig-zúr* (*tshig zur*) W. id.; rtág-par ma mtoṅ-bai tsig tos-nas (rtag par ma mthong ba'i tshig thos nas) always receiving the answer, that (she who was sought) had not been seen; tsig-med-par ₒgyúr-ba (tshig med par 'gyur ba) not being able to utter a word (from pain) Dzl.; but k'a-tsig-méd-par gsol-ba ₒdébs-pa (kha tshig med par gsol ba 'debs pa) Mil. prob. to pray without hypocrisy; tsig nyúṅ-la don čé-ba (tshig nyung la don che ba) Mil. saying much in few words; tsig-kyál-pa (tshig kyal pa) = kyal-ka (kyal ka) Dzl.; gžán-gyi tsig gčod-pa (gzhan gyi tshig gcod pa) to interrupt one in his speech; tsig-gsal (tshig gsal) a clear word, perspicuous style Cs.; tsig-ₒból (tshig 'bol) easy or fluent style Cs.; tsig-la mk'as-pa (tshig la mkhas pa) skilful in selecting words Cs.; bdén-tsig (bden tshig) v. bdén-pa (bden pa) extr.; brdzún-tsig (brdzun tshig) falsehood, lie Cs.

1. v. ₒtsíg-pa ('tshig pa). — 2. sbst., W. also tsíg-po (tshig po) anger, indignation, vexation, provocation, tsig-pa zá-ba (tshig pa za ba) to be angry Pth., frq.; *tsig(-po) k'ol* (*tshig (-po) khol*) W. his anger kindles.

less frq. tsigs-pa (tshigs pa), tsigs-ma (tshigs ma) 1. member between two joints, hence tsigs-mtsáms (tshigs mtshams) joint S.g.; joint, sor-tsigs (sor tshigs) the joints of the fingers, knuckles Cs.; tsigs ₒbúd-pa (tshigs 'bud pa) Cs., *t'úl-če* (*thul ce*), *bóg-če* (*bog ce*) W. to put out of joint, to dislocate, to sprain; tsigs ₒjúg-pa (tshigs 'jug pa) to reduce a dislocated joint Cs.; tsigs-nád (tshigs nad), tsigs-zúg (tshigs zug) articular disease, pain in the joints, gout; joint of the back-bone, vertebra; spine, also sgal-tsígs (sgal tshigs), vulgo tsigs-rús (tshigs rus), hence *tsig-gúr* (*tshig gur*) W. hump, hunch; joint, knee, knot, sog-tsígs (sog tshigs) knot of a stalk of corn or straw, smyug-tsígs (smyug tshigs) knot of cane Cs.; member of a generation Glr.; metrical division, verse, tsigs-su bčád-de smrá-ba (tshigs su bcad de smra ba) to speak in verse, tsigs(-su) bčad(-pa) (tshigs (-su) bcad (-pa)) strophe, stanza, tsigs-bčád byéd-pa (tshigs bcad byed pa) to compose verses, to speak in verse Dzl.; dus-tsígs (dus tshigs) division of time, e. g. season Pth. — 2. tsigs-ma (tshigs ma) sediment, residuum, residue, smán-gyi (sman gyi) of a medicine Dzl.; már-gyi (mar gyi) Dzl. olive-husks, oil-cake; tsigs-ró (tshigs ro) = tsígs-ma (tshigs ma).

vb. to be content; gen. adj. content, satisfied, satiated, consoled, frq.: yid tsim-par gyur (yid tshim par gyur) he was satisfied, appeased, consoled; ji ₒdód-pai yid tsím-ste (ji 'dod pa'i yid tshim ste) all her (their) wishes being satisfied Glr.; dga-bdés tsim-par gyúr-čiṅ (dga' bdes tshim par gyur cing) being indeed over-happy Pth.; tsim-par byéd-pa (tshim par byed pa) to satisfy, with the dat. or accus. of the person.
mig tsim-tsim ₒdug (mig tshim tshim 'dug) C. the eye is dazzled.

order, course, succession, turn, prob. only col., *ná-la tsir yoṅ* (*na la tshir yong*) or *bab* (*bab*) it is my turn; *ná-so tsír-la* (*na so tshir la*) succession by seniority; *gán-tsir żón-tsir* (*gan tshir zhon tshir*) id.; *tsir-la* (*tshir la*), *tsir-du* (*tshir du*), *tsir daṅ* (*tshir dang*) by turns, every one in his turn or course, one thing after the other.

fat, not melted, tsil-bu (tshil bu) id. S.g.; lúg-tsil (lug tshil) mutton fat, pág-tsil (pag tshil) pork-fat, bacon; kál-tsil (kal tshil), kóg-tsil (kog tshil), gród-tsil (grod tshil) suet, lard; sbó-tsil (sbo tshil) bacon; lóṅ-tsil (long tshil) intestinal fat. — spra-tsil (spra tshil) wax B., C. (W. *mum* (*mum*)); tsil-ku (tshil ku) liquid fat, in the living body, or melted fat Pth. — tsil-čan (tshil can), tsil-ldán (tshil ldan) fat, tsil-méd (tshil med) lean. — tsil-ró (tshil ro) remains of lard after melting. — tsil-šúbs (tshil shubs) 1. straight-gut, rectum Med. 2. sausage Cs. —
Mil., Thgy. prob. secondary form of rtsis (rtsis).
